2024-03-24.abstract: Indebted workers, facing brutal working conditions, are pushed to get hysterectomies as a treatment for routine ailments. Sugar mills disclaim responsibility.

2024-03-24.title: Senegal Votes in an Election That Almost Didn’t Happen
2024-03-24.abstract: The top opposition politician, Ousmane Sonko, is barred from running. So Sunday’s vote is widely seen as a choice between his handpicked candidate and the departing president’s designated successor.

2024-03-24.title: ISIS-K, Group Tied to Moscow Attack, Has Grown Bolder and More Violent
2024-03-24.abstract: The militant group violently opposes the Taliban leaders of Afghanistan, where it is based. It is increasingly targeting foreign foes.

2024-03-25.title: 4 Men Showing Signs of Beating Charged With Terrorism After Moscow Attack
2024-03-25.abstract: The four accused of carrying out an assault at a concert hall near Moscow are migrant laborers from Tajikistan. They face a maximum sentence of life in prison.

2024-03-25.title: To Live Past 100, Mangia a Lot Less: Italian Expert’s Ideas on Aging
2024-03-25.abstract: Valter Longo, who wants to live to a healthy 120 or 130, sees the key to longevity in diet — legumes and fish — and faux fasting.

2024-03-25.title: Waiters Compete in Paris’ Revived Cafe Race
2024-03-25.abstract: About 200 restaurant workers participated in the revived tradition, each one speeding through the course with a tray carrying a croissant, a glass of water and a coffee cup.

2024-03-25.title: The First Flight of Their Lives: An Airlift After Agony in Gaza
2024-03-25.abstract: The evacuees make up only a tiny fraction of the thousands of civilians, including many children, who have suffered grievous injuries over the course of Israel’s monthslong campaign against Hamas and its bombardment of Gaza.

2024-03-25.title: Putin Acknowledges ‘Radical Islamists’ Committed Moscow Attack
2024-03-25.abstract: But President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia held fast to the idea that a Western-backed Ukraine could have been the ultimate mastermind of the assault that killed 139 people.

2024-03-26.title: Display of Battered Men Was Russia’s Warning to the Public, Analysts Say
2024-03-26.abstract: Videos showing the torture of four men, accused of Russia’s deadliest terror attack in decades, have circulated widely in what analysts call a sign of the Russian state’s growing tolerance for public violence.

2024-03-26.title: A French-Malian Singer Is Caught in an Olympic Storm
2024-03-26.abstract: Aya Nakamura’s music is one of France’s top cultural exports. But reports that she might perform at the Paris Games have prompted fierce debates over identity and language.

2024-03-26.title: Who Could Sway the Outcome of the U.S. Election? Mexico’s President
2024-03-26.abstract: Immigration is a key issue for voters in the U.S. election, giving Mexico immense power to potentially shape the vote.

2024-03-26.title: Julian Assange Extradition Appeal Decision: What Could Happen?
2024-03-26.abstract: On Tuesday, two British judges will decide whether the WikiLeaks founder can appeal his extradition order to the United States.

2024-03-26.title: Why Are China’s Nationalists Attacking the Country’s Heroes?
2024-03-26.abstract: Online vitriol has targeted the country’s richest man, erasing billions of dollars of his company’s market value, despite Beijing’s courtship of entrepreneurs.

2024-03-27.title: Happy-Go-Lucky Australia Is Feeling Neither Happy, Nor Lucky
2024-03-27.abstract: After enjoying decades of prosperity, the country has hit stubborn economic turbulence.

2024-03-27.title: A Pivot to China Saved Elon Musk. It Also Binds Him to Beijing.
2024-03-27.abstract: Tesla and China built a symbiotic relationship, with credits, workers and parts that made Mr. Musk ultrarich. Now, his reliance on the country may give Beijing leverage.

2024-03-27.title: How Elon Musk Became ‘Kind of Pro-China’
2024-03-27.abstract: Mr. Musk helped create China’s electric vehicle industry. But he is now facing challenges there as well as scrutiny in the West over his reliance on the country.

2024-03-27.title: Inside the Garrick, the Elite Men-Only London Club Rocked by Criticism
2024-03-27.abstract: Founded in 1831, the opulent private club has long guarded its membership list closely. A leak this month caused a scandal.

2024-03-27.title: Thailand Lawmakers Bring Same-Sex Marriage a Crucial Step Closer
2024-03-27.abstract: The House of Representatives voted overwhelmingly to legalize same-sex marriage. The bill now goes to Thailand’s Senate.

2024-03-28.title: One Satellite Signal Rules Modern Life. What if Someone Knocks It Out?
2024-03-28.abstract: Threats are mounting in space. GPS signals are vulnerable to attack. Their time-keeping is essential for stock trading, power transmission and more.

2024-03-28.title: From Village to Prison to Africa’s Youngest Elected President
2024-03-28.abstract: How did Bassirou Diomaye Faye, age 44, go from obscurity to a resounding win in Senegal’s presidential election? At the family homestead, one relative explained, “This family is not new to ruling.”

2024-03-28.title: Why Russia’s Vast Security Services Fell Short on Deadly Attack
2024-03-28.abstract: The factors behind the failure to prevent a terrorist attack include a distrust of foreign intelligence, a focus on Ukraine and a distracting political crackdown at home.

2024-03-28.title: South Korea’s Parliament Election 2024: What You Need to Know
2024-03-28.abstract: Results for the Assembly-controlling opposition party are likely to be a referendum on President Yoon Suk Yeol’s two years in office.

2024-03-28.title: The Japanese Sensei Bringing Baseball to Brazil
2024-03-28.abstract: Once a semipro baseball player in Japan, Yukihiro Shimura has now become a baseball missionary.

2024-03-29.title: A River Awakens, Bringing Green Magic to a Desert Town
2024-03-29.abstract: After a stunning week of rain, the Todd River in Alice Springs flows once again, bringing a beleaguered community together.

2024-03-29.title: A Very Royal Scavenger Hunt
2024-03-29.abstract: Taking part in mass online sleuthing can feel thrilling. But the same impulses can take a dark turn.

2024-03-29.title: From New England to Notre-Dame, a U.S. Carpenter Tends to a French Icon
2024-03-29.abstract: Hank Silver, a timber framer based in Massachusetts, is one of a handful of foreigners who are helping to rebuild the Paris cathedral after the devastating fire in 2019.

2024-03-29.title: A Harsh Mongolian Winter Leaves Millions of Livestock Dead
2024-03-29.abstract: Mass death caused by a weather event known in Mongolia as dzud has devastated herds, leaving thousands of families short of food.

2024-03-29.title: Wanted in South Korea: Imperialism-Free Cherry Blossoms
2024-03-29.abstract: Activists want to replace a variety of cherry tree associated with the Japanese colonial era with one they say is Korean. The science is messy.

2024-03-30.title: A 500-Year Old Chapel, 438 Solar Panels and an Architectural Squabble
2024-03-30.abstract: King’s College Chapel in Cambridge is one of several British landmarks that have installed solar panels, gratifying climate campaigners and ruffling feathers among some traditionalists.

2024-03-30.title: A Stork, a Fisherman and Their Unlikely Bond Enchant Turkey
2024-03-30.abstract: Thirteen years ago, a stork landed on a fisherman’s boat looking for food. He has come back every year since, drawing national attention.

2024-03-30.title: Dispute Over Conscription for Ultra-Orthodox Jews Presents New Threat to Netanyahu
2024-03-30.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s cabinet is divided about whether ultra-Orthodox Jews should be required to join the Israeli army.

2024-03-30.title: The ‘Night Government’ Expands Its Violent Reach in Rohingya Camps
2024-03-30.abstract: Gunfights, kidnappings and homicides have become widespread in the refugee settlements in Bangladesh, as armed groups and criminal gangs have become more brazen.

2024-03-30.title: Watch Turkey’s Famous Stork on Live Webcam
2024-03-30.abstract: A 24-hour webcam allows stork lovers worldwide to watch Yaren, who returns to visit his human friend every year.

2024-03-31.title: Angry Farmers Are Reshaping Europe
2024-03-31.abstract: Farm protests are changing not only Europe’s food system but also its politics, as the far right senses an opportunity.

2024-03-31.title: India’s Silicon Valley Faces a Water Crisis That Software Cannot Solve
2024-03-31.abstract: Bengaluru gets plenty of rain. But the city did not properly adapt as its soaring population strained traditional water sources.

2024-03-31.title: At Stake in the Istanbul Mayoral Race: Turkey’s Political Future
2024-03-31.abstract: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan has campaigned for his party to retake Istanbul from the incumbent mayor, whom many see as a potential future president.

2024-03-31.title: A Russian Defector’s Killing Raises Specter of Hit Squads
2024-03-31.abstract: The death in Spain of Maksim Kuzminov, a pilot who delivered a helicopter and secret documents to Ukraine, has raised fears that the Kremlin is again targeting its enemies.

2024-03-31.title: In Yemen, Renewed Conflict and Rising Hunger Stalk a Lean Ramadan
2024-03-31.abstract: Airstrikes, crippling inflation and a drop in foreign aid are raising alarms about a new humanitarian crisis in the world’s poorest Arab country.
